topStories.title: UAE accuses Iran of missile and drone attacks, Strait of Hormuz tensions reignite
topStories.description: The attacks followed a new Trump-led maritime initiative aimed at restoring commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz after months of disruptions.

topStories.title: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
topStories.description: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

topStories.title: Judge apologizes to alleged would-be Trump assassin, drags Jan. 6 suspects into rant over jail complaints
topStories.description: A federal judge decried the prison treatment of alleged Trump would-be assassin Cole Allen and apologized for his confinement on suicide watch.

topStories.title: Feds open probe into New York City's anti-Israel teachers
topStories.description: NYC Public Schools faces a Trump administration civil rights investigation over alleged antisemitic teachings by a group of anti-Israel educators.

topStories.title: House minority leader mocks Trump with 'Jeffries Derangement Syndrome' jab
topStories.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, a Democrat from New York, fired back at President Donald Trump's attacks by accusing the president of "Jeffries Derangement Syndrome."

topStories.title: Oil Jumps as Violence Flares After Trump’s Offer to Help Ships Through Strait
topStories.description: Oil prices fell and futures on the S&P 500 rose slightly on Sunday as investors reacted to continued uncertainty about the war in Iran.

topStories.title: Rubio to Visit Rome After Trump’s Feud With the Pope and Meloni
topStories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s trip will come after President Trump criticized Pope Leo XIV and then turned on Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy for defending the pontiff.

topStories.title: Mark Carney Pulls Canada Closer to Europe as Both Struggle With Trump
topStories.description: At a summit of European leaders, the Canadian prime minister was a special guest offering deals and friendship to jittery allies.

topStories.title: Germans Are Not Panicking Over Trump’s Troop Threats
topStories.description: Top leaders believe losing 5,000 U.S. troops will not hurt German security. But analysts fear an economic hit, especially in military communities.

dailyNews.title: Trump vs History: How president's poll numbers compare to Biden, Obama, Bush ahead of midterms
dailyNews.description: It may come as cold comfort to the White House, but Trump's underwater approval ratings echo a pattern seen with Biden, Obama and Bush before midterms.

dailyNews.title: U.S. sinks 6 small Iranian boats in Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM says
dailyNews.description: The U.S. destroyed six small Iranian boats in the Strait of Hormuz, Central Command said Monday. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean, retired U.S. Navy Adm. Robert Murrett and CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro join with more.

dailyNews.title: Bison Have Grazed These Lands for Centuries. Trump Wants to Evict Them.
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is evicting bison herds from federal grasslands in Montana, siding with ranchers and Republican leaders over environmentalists and tribal leaders.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Orders Rapid End to Hunting Regulations on Federal Lands
dailyNews.description: Internal Interior Department documents show parks are imposing sweeping changes like expanding areas where firearms can by used and allowing game to be cleaned in restrooms.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Sues Minnesota to Block Climate Lawsuit
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department is seeking to block a lawsuit filed against major players in the fossil fuel industry over their role in climate change.

dailyNews.title: Trump family's World Liberty Financial sues billionaire for defamation
dailyNews.description: The lawsuit ramps up a feud between World Liberty Financial and Sun, who last month sued the Trump-backed company for fraud.

dailyNews.title: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect moved off suicide watch ahead of court appearance
dailyNews.description: Cole Allen, the suspected shooter at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, was moved off suicide watch in jail ahead of his appearance in court on Monday. CBS News senior coordinating producer Anna Schecter has more.

dailyNews.title: Two U.S. merchant vessels successfully transit Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM says
dailyNews.description: Two U.S. merchant vessels successfully transited through the Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM said, after President Trump announced a plan called "Project Freedom," where the U.S. will guide ships through the chokepoint. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean and Senior White House correspondent Nancy Cordes have more.

dailyNews.title: Trump attack defendant Cole Allen under harsher jail conditions than Jan. 6 defendants: judge
dailyNews.description: Cole Allen is charged with trying to assassinate President Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington Hilton Hotel.

dailyNews.title: Trump said $465,000 in retirement savings is 'rich.' Is it?
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump said savers who build a $465,000 nest egg would be "rich." Whether that's true depends on one's perception of wealth.

dailyNews.title: Iran attacks U.S. ships in the Strait of Hormuz amid ‘Project Freedom’
dailyNews.description: The commander of the U.S. Central Command said Iran carried out a cruise missile and drone attack on U.S. military ships and targeted commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The attacks come after President Donald Trump announced “Project Freedom,” aimed at guiding ships through the strait. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports on how the U.S. will respond to Iran and carry out its plan.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will guide ships through Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Trump says U.S. will guide ships through Strait of Hormuz

dailyNews.title: Senator says Trump's actions violates war powers law
dailyNews.description: Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California says that while multiple presidents have not gone to Congress for war authorization in the past, President Trump’s actions are a “clear violation” of the War Powers Act of 1973.

dailyNews.title: Sen. Reed on Trump’s Iran strategy: ‘I don’t believe the president has a plan’
dailyNews.description: Ranking Senate Armed Services Committee Democrat Jack Reed said he believes the U.S. is in a "much worse position" with Iran since the war started.
